stimulus: When it was first described by European naturalists in 1828, the spiny, egg-laying echidna so confounded zoological taxonomy that one researcher proposed the name Tachyglossus aculeatus: "swift-tongued spiny one." The mammal, which lays eggs but also feeds ______ young with milk secreted through the skin, has since been placed alongside the platypus in the order Monotremata.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The singular possessive pronoun "its" agrees with the singular antecedent "the mammal" and indicates that the "young" belong to it.

Choice A is incorrect because "they're" is the contraction of "they are," producing both a pronoun-antecedent agreement error with the singular antecedent "the mammal" and an extra verb in a clause that already has the main verb "feeds." Choice C is incorrect because "their" is plural and does not agree with the singular antecedent "the mammal." Choice D is incorrect because "it's" is the contraction of "it is," introducing an extra verb into a clause that already has the main verb "feeds."
